description: The Zemun Clan is a Serbian mafia group based in Zemun, a municipality of Belgrade. Founded by Dušan Spasojević and Mile Luković in the 1990s, it rose to power between 1999 and 2003, becoming one of the most powerful organizations in Southeast Europe. The clan engaged in a wide range of criminal activities including drug trafficking, murder, and racketeering.

country_links.evidence_quote: In November 2009, Argentine Police arrested 5 Serbian drug couriers and seized their 492 kilograms of cocaine in Buenos Aires.
country_links.context: The Zemun Clan's drug couriers were arrested in Argentina with cocaine.

country_links.evidence_quote: The routes of the drugs were from Uruguay and Argentina to South Africa to Northern Italy, Turkey and Montenegro.
country_links.context: The Zemun Clan's drug routes go to Northern Italy.
